title: phagnalon saxatile subsp. tenorei
intro: Phagnalon saxatile subsp. tenorei, also known as Tenore's Phagnalon, is a plant species in the daisy family (Asteraceae) that is native to the Mediterranean region. This plant is used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and protect against oxidative stress. The health benefits of Phagnalon saxatile subsp. tenorei include its ability to aid in the treatment of gastrointestinal disorders, respiratory infections, and skin conditions, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Herbal preparations of Phagnalon saxatile subsp. tenorei can be found in the form of teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to promote overall well-being and support the body's natural healing processes.
